base_runtime.note: The dataset branch is stacked on the current leakage-remediated runtime. This authoring file contains Gold and must never be passed directly to the candidate executor.

allowed_alternatives: A maintained Hugging Face BERT implementation may be used operationally, but must not be mislabeled as the original official repository.

baseline_decision.canonical: BERT-base classifier with a linear intent head

compatibility_judgment.reject: Saying LoRA is compatible merely because both systems use Transformers.
required_checks: Inject trainable low-rank matrices into existing dense query/value projections without changing token order.
Freeze original BERT weights and verify only LoRA plus classifier parameters receive gradients.
Preserve attention mask, hidden size, residual path, tokenizer, and classification-head semantics.
Record rank, alpha scaling, dropout, target modules, and merge/unmerge behavior.

expected_assets.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

experiments: Report macro-F1, accuracy, per-intent recall, OOS AUROC/FPR95, trainable parameters, peak memory, and time
gap_analysis: Full fine-tuning all BERT parameters with 10 examples per class can overfit and has high variance.
LoRA reduces trainable parameters but does not by itself solve OOS detection or class imbalance.
The original LoRA paper validates several Transformer families, but a BERT intent-classification integration still needs its own contract and experiment.
hard_failures: Calling a third-party PEFT wrapper the official LoRA repository.
Comparing different tuning budgets without disclosure.
minimal_method: Start from a verified BERT-base intent classifier.
Add LoRA to query and value projections only.
Train LoRA matrices plus classification head.
Keep a switch producing exact frozen/full-fine-tune baselines.
Treat OOS scoring as a separate calibrated head or score, not an automatic consequence of LoRA.
gold.reference_hypothesis: For 10-shot CLINC150, adaptation updates are approximately low-rank in selected self-attention projections. LoRA on query and value projections should match or improve macro-F1 over full fine-tuning at lower trainable-parameter and optimizer-memory cost, without degrading OOS AUROC beyond a predefined guardrail.
stop_conditions: LoRA gain disappears over multiple few-shot samplings.
Full fine-tuning wins under equal search budget and memory is not a project constraint.
OOS performance degrades beyond the guardrail.
Gradient audit shows frozen BERT parameters are updated.

task_definition.dataset_note: CLINC150/OOS contains in-scope intents and out-of-scope utterances; class and OOS protocols must remain distinct.
task_definition.task: Few-shot, 150-way in-domain intent classification with explicit out-of-scope evaluation.

supplied_materials.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models

public_input.user_input: Baseline 是论文《BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding》。平行论文《LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models》通过低秩增量矩阵进行参数高效适配。请判断能否把 LoRA 接入 BERT，用于 CLINC150 的每类 10-shot 意图分类，并设计方法和实验。

allowed_alternatives: A simpler tile-selection or coarse-to-fine cascade is acceptable if it is compared against the same fixed Oriented R-CNN protocol.

baseline_decision.canonical: Oriented R-CNN on a fixed DOTA split and tile policy

compatibility_judgment.reject: Copying the QueryDet detection head wholesale and calling it compatible with oriented proposals.
required_checks: Query targets must represent candidate spatial regions, not axis-aligned final boxes.
Sparse features must preserve spatial coordinates required by oriented RPN/RoIAlign.
Rotated box parameterization, feature levels, stride, and NMS/merge semantics remain baseline-consistent.
Sparse operators and dense fallback must produce equivalent outputs on fully selected regions.

expected_assets.role: official oriented aerial object detection dataset
expected_assets.title: DOTA
QueryDet: Cascaded Sparse Query for Accelerating High-Resolution Small Object Detection

experiments: Sparse routing at one FPN level
Oracle routing upper bound using ground-truth regions
Report DOTA mAP, AP by object size/category, recall, duplicate detections, FLOPs, wall-clock latency, peak memory, and selected-region ratio
gap_analysis: High-resolution feature computation is expensive over large background regions.
QueryDet's sparse query mechanism was designed around feature-pyramid one-stage detection, not Oriented R-CNN's oriented RPN/RoI path.
DOTA results are highly sensitive to crop size, overlap, multiscale testing, and merge/NMS policy.
hard_failures: Using axis-aligned AP as the sole target metric.
Comparing different DOTA tiling/merge policies as model gains.
Claiming QueryDet directly supports oriented R-CNN without interface analysis.
minimal_method: Freeze Oriented R-CNN and DOTA tiling/merge protocol.
Add a low-resolution objectness query head.
Use it only to gate selected high-resolution FPN locations.
Keep oriented RPN and oriented RoI head unchanged.
Provide a dense-mode switch for parity.
gold.reference_hypothesis: Small oriented objects occupy sparse regions of high-resolution aerial tiles. A coarse query head that selects regions for high-resolution feature refinement may reduce effective high-resolution computation while preserving small-object oriented AP, but only if the sparse routing is integrated before oriented proposal/refinement without changing tile and merge protocols.
stop_conditions: Sparse path changes crop/overlap or test-time augmentation relative to baseline.
Accuracy gain comes from higher effective resolution rather than routing.
Latency is not measured on the target stack.
Sparse operator cannot preserve oriented-coordinate semantics.

task_definition.task: Oriented object detection on tiled high-resolution aerial images.

public_input.user_input: 以论文《Oriented R-CNN for Object Detection》为 Baseline，在 DOTA 密集小目标场景中降低高分辨率推理成本，同时保持旋转框检测精度。

allowed_alternatives: Use SMD/SMAP/MSL for code-accessible replication, but do not relabel them as industrial SWaT evidence.

baseline_decision.canonical: USAD under a frozen SWaT preprocessing, windowing, threshold, and event-scoring protocol

direct_graft_failure: USAD latent semantics and Anomaly Transformer's temporal-token semantics differ.
Association discrepancy depends on prior/series associations and minimax optimization.
A reshape or added attention layer does not preserve the original mechanism.
compatibility_judgment.permitted_adapter: A separate temporal-attention branch trained with its own verified objective, followed by validation-only score calibration/fusion.

expected_assets.title: USAD: UnSupervised Anomaly Detection on Multivariate Time Series
Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

expected_assets.role: official industrial water-treatment dataset; access terms apply
expected_assets.title: SWaT

experiments: Report point-wise and event-wise precision/recall/F1, AUROC/AUPRC, detection delay, false alarms per hour, and results with and without point adjustment
gap_analysis: USAD is an autoencoder/adversarial reconstruction architecture, not an attention encoder.
Association discrepancy is defined jointly with Anomaly-Attention and a minimax training objective.
Extracting only an attention tensor or discrepancy score would remove the mechanism's training contract.
hard_failures: Claiming association discrepancy is a standalone attention layer.
Ignoring minimax training and prior/series association semantics.
Using test labels for threshold or fusion tuning.
Claiming SWaT data are bundled in the repository when access is controlled.
minimal_method: Freeze USAD reconstruction score and threshold protocol.
Train an independent Anomaly Transformer branch on the same training windows.
Normalize scores using training/validation data only.
Evaluate score fusion with a predeclared coefficient.
Do not claim an integrated USAD architecture until the branch independently helps.
gold.reference_hypothesis: A direct internal graft of Anomaly-Attention into USAD is not a minimal module transfer because it changes the encoder family and objective. A bounded pilot may instead compare a separate association-discrepancy branch or score-level fusion against USAD reconstruction, testing whether complementary temporal association evidence improves event-level detection under an unchanged protocol.
stop_conditions: Direct graft cannot state a complete loss and gradient contract.
Fusion coefficient is tuned on the test set.
Gain exists only under point adjustment.
Independent Anomaly Transformer does not add complementary error coverage.

task_definition.protocol_warning: Point adjustment and threshold selection can dominate reported scores and must be fixed before comparison.
task_definition.task: Unsupervised multivariate time-series anomaly detection on SWaT.

supplied_materials.title: USAD: UnSupervised Anomaly Detection on Multivariate Time Series
Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy

public_input.user_input: Baseline 是论文《USAD: UnSupervised Anomaly Detection on Multivariate Time Series》。平行论文《Anomaly Transformer: Time Series Anomaly Detection with Association Discrepancy》提出 Anomaly-Attention 和 association discrepancy。请判断能否将该机制接入 USAD，用于 SWaT 工业控制系统异常检测。

allowed_alternatives: Amazon review metadata may replace MovieLens if licenses, timestamps, content availability, and preprocessing are fully verified.

baseline_decision.canonical: SASRec for warm/short-history sequential behavior, paired with a content-aware cold-start baseline such as DropoutNet
baseline_decision.reason: SASRec alone cannot recommend truly unseen item IDs without a content-to-item representation path.

recommended: Map item content/tag features to the same semantic space as SASRec item embeddings.
During training, randomly replace some collaborative item embeddings with content-derived embeddings.
At inference, unseen items use content-derived embeddings.
compatibility_judgment.reject: Claiming SASRec handles cold-start items by merely shortening user histories.
required_checks: Content features are available before the item first appears.
No future interaction statistics enter content features.
Item ID, padding ID, and mask semantics remain distinct.

expected_assets.title: BERT4Rec: Sequential Recommendation with Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformer
DropoutNet: Addressing Cold Start in Recommender Systems

experiments: SASRec with random embedding initialization for cold items
Warm, short-history-user, and cold-item strata reported separately
BERT4Rec as strong sequential comparison under equal data/tuning budget
Report Recall@K, NDCG@K, MRR, coverage, warm/cold gap, and latency
gap_analysis: ID-only SASRec has no learned embedding for a new item.
Short-history users have weak sequential context.
MovieLens stable datasets contain ratings/tags, but cold-start splits must be constructed without temporal or identity leakage.
Warm-start metrics can conceal complete failure on cold items.
hard_failures: Treating random holdout as a true new-item cold-start test.
Claiming ID-only SASRec can score unseen item IDs without an embedding construction path.
Leaking future tags/interactions into cold-item content.
Reporting only aggregate metrics that hide cold strata.
Calling MovieLens latest mutable data a stable research benchmark.
minimal_method: Freeze SASRec warm baseline and chronological split.
Train a content-to-item embedding mapper.
Evaluate content-only cold-item recommendation.
Add embedding dropout/replacement during SASRec training.
Only then add a separate short-history user adaptation.
gold.reference_hypothesis: A content encoder trained to predict or initialize the SASRec item embedding, combined with item-embedding dropout inspired by DropoutNet, should improve Recall@K and NDCG@K on temporally held-out cold items while preserving warm-item performance. A short-history user branch may use pooled content/history features, but should be evaluated separately.
stop_conditions: Cold items appear in training interactions.
Content/tag features use future interaction-derived statistics.
Improvement is only on warm users/items.
SASRec/BERT4Rec comparison uses unequal convergence budgets.

task_definition.required_distinction: Warm sequential recommendation, new-item cold start, and new/short-history user evaluation are separate protocols.
task_definition.task: Next-item recommendation under item cold start and short-history user conditions.

public_input.user_input: 带内容侧信息的冷启动序列推荐：同时处理新物品和短历史用户

forbidden: Reading gold.expected_assets, gold.baseline_decision, gold.reference_hypothesis, gold.compatibility_judgment, gold.experiments, or gold.hard_failures during candidate execution.
Branching production behavior on case_id, domain labels, paper titles, or benchmark-specific topic tables.
Treating repository existence as baseline reproduction.
Treating shape alignment as semantic compatibility.
execution_boundary.required_projection: Before execution, create a gold-independent BenchmarkInput containing only public_input.user_input plus supplied paper titles and user-declared roles.

rubric.scoring_policy: Use fact anchors and role-bound evidence, not exact-string matching. A hard failure makes the case fail regardless of soft score.
